title: Get started with lagdynamics
text: LSA long history behavioral, educational, interaction research, applied studies often stop description. Observed transition rates reported, plotted, interpreted, inferential question left implicit: whether apparent pattern larger expected chance sequential pairing. Existing tooling also tended dated, object-centric, difficult combine modern data workflows. lagdynamics modernizes practice. implements lag-sequential analysis tidy, visual, interoperable, confirmatory workflow. result returned verb one-row-per-observation data frame. fitted model can read, plotted, resampled, compared across groups, converted network objects without indexing internal slots. design follows Dynalytics framework Saqr, Lopez-Pernas, Misiejuk (2026). title: Get started with lagdynamics
text: One plotting verb covers main views: plot(fit, type = ). default heatmap displays adjusted residuals, makes direct visual form LSA model. residual network displays departures directed edges. probability-weighted network switches representation TNA transition network. Chord sunburst views summarize transition flow outgoing distributions. Uncertainty forests group barrels produced plot() validation comparison results.      See vignette("plotting", package = "lagdynamics") full plotting gallery.
code: plot(fit)                     # residual heatmap (default) plot(fit, type = "network")   # residual network plot(fit, type = "network", weights = "prob")   # TNA transition network plot(fit, type = "chord") plot(fit, type = "sunburst")

title: Get started with lagdynamics

Wide data frames matrices use rows sequences columns ordered positions. shape bundled data sets. canonical sequence representation raw transition table can also inspected directly. helpers useful sequencing contract needs made explicit model fitted. title: Get started with lagdynamics
text: adjusted residual tests whether edge departs independence fitted table. Stronger claims require evidence. lagdynamics therefore includes confirmatory battery follows Dynalytics contract. certainty_lsa() gives analytic Bayesian uncertainty transition probabilities. uses Dirichlet-Multinomial posterior source state’s outgoing transitions returns credible intervals without resampling. bootstrap_lsa() resampling counterpart. resamples whole sequences, refits model, summarizes edge-level variability. forest plot displays interval evidence selected edge metric.  reliability_lsa() estimates whole-network reproducibility repeated split-half refitting. stability_lsa() drops cases records whether edges remain significant. permute_lsa() constructs empirical null shuffling events within sequences recomputing residuals. Group comparison confirmatory rather visual. compare_lsa() permutes group labels sequence level tests whether transition structures differ. bayes_compare_lsa() analytic Bayesian comparison: reports plausible range group differences transition probabilities.

title: Compare Groups' Transition Structures — compare_lsa
text: x Either lsa_group object (lsa(..., group = )) two groups, single lsa fit first group. y x single lsa fit, second group's lsa fit. Ignored (must NULL) x lsa_group. R Integer. Number label permutations per comparison. Default 1000. measure Character. per-edge quantity compared groups. Default "log_or": per-cell log odds ratio 2x2 transition collapse (Haldane-Anscombe corrected empty cells) – N-invariant LSA effect size, comparison reflects behaviour rather sample size. options: "yules_q" (also N-invariant, saturates +/-1 zero cells), "adj_res" (adjusted residuals – LSA test statistic, scales sqrt(N) therefore confounded group size; message warns groups differ size), "prob" (transition probabilities – raw rate, .e. TNA quantity, independence baseline), "count", "lift" (observed / expected). adjust Multiple-comparison correction; method accepted stats::p.adjust() (e.g. "holm", "BH", "bonferroni"). Default "none". two groups applied across pooled per-edge p-values pairs. min_count Integer. Minimum pooled observed count (group + group b) transition tested. Default 5. Rarer cells carry unstable odds ratio near-degenerate permutation null produces spurious small p-values, get p = NA excluded multiple-comparison family omnibus rather flagged significant. Set 0 test every cell. parallel Logical. Use multi-core resampling. Default FALSE. n_cores Integer. Worker count parallel = TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress every 100 permutations. ... Reserved. NA handling. Non-estimable cells (structural zeros, zero-margin rows permuted pseudo-group) carry NA measure matrix never coerced zero. cells get p_perm = NA rather spurious significant flag, exceedance tally omnibus statistic computed na.rm = TRUE, matching permute_lsa(). Interpretation caveats. odds ratio non-collapsible: per-group log odds ratios group-specific departure-- independence measures pooled across groups different marginal state distributions. title: Lag Sequential Analysis — lsa_classical
text: data Sequence input (form accepted lsa_data()), raw long-format event-log data.frame actor / action arguments supplied (see ). Accepted already- sequenced forms include vectors, lists sequences, wide matrices/data.frames, transition-count matrices, sequence- bearing objects (tna, group_tna, nestimate_data, stslist). NA empty-string cells treated missingness, state: dropped wherever occur transition counted . model missingness state, recode (e.g. NA -> "missing") calling lsa(). ... Additional engine-specific parameters (merged params). lag Integer. transition lag. Default 1. Positive lags count successors (state t -> t + lag); negative lags count predecessors (occurred |lag| steps ); 0 pairs event (degenerate single-stream event data – genuine co-occurrence needs concurrent codes, yet supported). Pre-computed transition-matrix input supports lag = 1 . analyse several lags , see lsa_lags(). engine Character scalar. engine name, registered via register_lsa_engine(). Built-engines: "classical", "two_cell", "bidirectional", "parallel_dominance", "nonparallel_dominance". Default "classical". alternative Character scalar. alternative hypothesis adjusted-residual kappa p-values: one "two.sided" (default), "greater", "less". alpha Numeric. Significance threshold used mark edges significant fit$edges$significant. Default 0.05. loops Logical. Keep self-transitions (diagonal)? Default TRUE. Set loops = FALSE forbid every self-transition – common reason exclude cells – without building matrix hand. structural_zeros Optional K x K 0/1 matrix arbitrary forbidden-cell pattern, 0 marks forbidden (structural-zero) cell 1 estimable one. Default NULL: every cell part model. Combines loops: loops = FALSE also zeros diagonal supplied matrix. cell forbidden engine switches iterative proportional fitting Christensen's design-matrix residuals (see inst/REFERENCES.md §2.2, §4.2). labels Optional character vector state labels. group Optional grouping multi-group fit. Either vector one entry per input sequence (length n_sequences), — long-format input (see actor/action) — name grouping column log, must constant within actor/session recovered sequence maps one group. Sequences partitioned group separate lsa fit built . group fits share one global label set (derived full data) K x K matrices directly comparable, even group never visits state. Returns lsa_group object (named list lsa fits). Requires event-level input; pre-computed transition matrix split group. Default NULL (single-group fit). actor, action, time, order, session Column names (single string) long-format event-log input. Supplying action (actor) switches lsa() long-format mode: raw log data sequenced event sequences grouping rows per actor (optionally crossed explicit session id), ordering within group order given else time, – time given session column – starting new session whenever gap consecutive events exceeds time_threshold seconds. NULL default (input taken already-sequenced). combined group. time_threshold Numeric. Maximum gap seconds consecutive events new session started long-format mode. Default 900 (15 minutes). Ignored unless time given session . custom_format Optional strptime format string parsing time column (e.g. "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"). Default NULL (native date/time classes ISO strings parsed directly). is_unix_time Logical. Treat time column Unix epoch. Default FALSE. unix_time_unit Character. Unit Unix epoch is_unix_time = TRUE: "seconds" (default), "milliseconds", "microseconds". params Optional named list engine-specific parameters forwarded engine function.

title: Lag Sequential Analysis — lsa_classical
text: object class c("lsa", "cograph_network"). Read verbs rather reaching slots: transitions() tidy edge table, nodes(), tests(), initial(), summary() results, plot()/plot_transitions() draw . Every number verb returns backed slots: edges tidy one-row-per-transition frame backs transitions() (extra cograph_network protocol columns). nodes Data frame backing nodes(): id, label, name, outgoing, incoming. obs, exp, prob, prob_col, adj_res, p, yules_q, kappa, kappa_z, kappa_p per-cell quantities edges, K x K matrix form (prob row-conditional P(| ), prob_col column-conditional P(| )). Convenient matrix algebra; primary interface. lrx2, x2 Lists (statistic, df, p) backing tests(): tablewise likelihood-ratio (G^2) Pearson chi-square tests independence; NULL engines without expected table. inits Named numeric vector backing initial() (proportion sequences starting state, sums 1); NULL transition-matrix input. weights K x K matrix used default edge weight plotting. Equal obs (counts) default. directed Logical scalar; TRUE directed engines, FALSE bidirectional. method Engine name (slot cograph_network protocol reads). Also recorded params$engine. data canonical lsa_data object (events + seq_id). params Immutable snapshot parameters used (recipe), including params$engine. meta List source, IPF info, version, call. group supplied, returns object class c("lsa_group", "list"): named list lsa fits (one per group level) carrying levels, group_sizes, labels, engine attributes. Downstream verbs (transitions(), transition_probabilities(), reliability_lsa(), etc.) dispatch return grouped results.

what: Permutation Test for an LSA Fit — permute_lsa
title: Permutation Test for an LSA Fit — permute_lsa
text: Empirical null-distribution p-values every cell LSA transition matrix. Repeatedly shuffles input event vector (within sequence boundaries) recomputes engine's residual matrix, producing permutation distribution cell. two-sided p-value (1 + #{ |stat_perm| >= |stat_obs| }) / (1 + R) (Phipson & Smyth 2010).

title: Permutation Test for an LSA Fit — permute_lsa
code: permute_lsa(   fit,   R = 1000L,   within_sequence = TRUE,   shuffles = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Permutation Test for an LSA Fit — permute_lsa
text: fit lsa object returned lsa(). R Integer. Number permutations. Default 1000. within_sequence Logical. TRUE (default multi-sequence input), sequence shuffled independently; FALSE, whole event stream shuffled across sequence boundaries. shuffles Optional list length R, element integer permutation seq_len(n_events). supplied, replaces internal RNG. parallel Logical. Use multi-core resampling. Default FALSE. n_cores Integer. Worker count parallel = TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress every 100 replicates. ... Reserved. NA handling. exceedance count drives p_perm computed na.rm = TRUE, replicates produced NA cell (structural-zero cells, zero-margin cells permuted table) excluded cell's tally rather counted either exceedance non-exceedance.

title: Permutation Test for an LSA Fit — permute_lsa
text: object class c("lsa_permutation", "list") : edges Tidy per-edge data frame observed counts residuals, empirical permutation p-value p_perm, significant flag recipe's alpha threshold. perm_adj_res R x K^2 numeric matrix permuted residuals (cells .vector(adj_res) order). R, within_sequence Recipe metadata. fit Reference original fit.

title: Permutation Test for an LSA Fit — permute_lsa
text: Castellan, N. J. (1992). Shuffling arrays: appearances may deceiving. Behavior Research Methods, Instruments, & Computers, 24(1), 72-77. Phipson, B., & Smyth, G. K. (2010). Permutation p-values never zero: calculating exact p-values permutations randomly drawn. Statistical Applications Genetics Molecular Biology, 9(1), Article 39.

title: Plot the Transition Network — plot_transitions
code: plot_transitions(   fit,   weights = c("residuals", "count", "prob", "lift", "yules_q"),   significant = FALSE,   top = NULL,   decimals = 1,   node_fill = "white",   edge_labels = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot the Transition Network — plot_transitions
text: fit lsa fit lsa(). weights matrix becomes edge weight, drawn: "residuals" (default) – residual network (transition one): adjusted residuals coloured sign TNA / Nestimate convention, blue = (-represented) solid red = less (avoided) dashed soft halo. "prob" / "count" – familiar transition network Transition Network Analysis (TNA), drawn cograph::splot(tna_styling = TRUE): cograph's TNA styling (coloured nodes, weighted directed edges) plus donut ring per node carrying initial-state probability, edges labelled transition probability ("prob") observed count ("count"). "prob", edges 0.05 dropped default weak transitions clutter plot (override edge_cutoff). "lift" – observed / expected, drawn single neutral colour magnitude carried edge width. "yules_q" – signed association network: Yule's Q fixed [-1, 1] scale, coloured sign like residual network (blue -represented, red avoided) bounded growing sample size. significant Logical. Keep edges whose adjusted-residual p-value fit's alpha; weaker cells set 0 (edge). Default FALSE. Note large sample sizes almost every cell significant, weak visual filter – prefer top (effect-size pruning) declutter dense residual network. top Numeric NULL. Keep strongest edges absolute weight (applied significant); rest set 0. fraction 0 < top < 1 keeps proportion present edges (top = 0.5 -> strongest half); value top >= 1 keeps many edges (top = 12 -> 12 strongest). legible way thin dense residual network: prunes effect size (|adjusted residual|) rather p-value. NULL (default) keeps every edge. Applies every view; probability network composes default edge_cutoff = 0.05. decimals Number decimal places edge labels. Default 1. node_fill Node fill colour. Default "white"; probability / count networks use per-state palette instead unless node_fill set explicitly. edge_labels Logical (label vector). Show edge weights labels. Default TRUE. ... Passed cograph::splot() (e.g. node_shape, layout, edge_cutoff, curvature).

what: Directed transfer entropy for categorical sequences (experimental) — transfer_entropy
title: Directed transfer entropy for categorical sequences (experimental) — transfer_entropy
text: Experimental. Transfer entropy (Schreiber, 2000) measures directed predictive coupling: much knowing source's past reduces uncertainty target's future, beyond target's past already explains. conditions target's history, transfer entropy immune autocorrelation confound inflates plain lagged association process strong momentum. Unlike lsa()'s Yule's Q / adjusted residuals, transfer entropy sign-blind: large value means "strong directed predictive structure", may facilitating suppressing. Read alongside signed measures transitions() interpret direction effect. Two modes: State-flow network (default, y = NULL): given categorical sequences, returns directed transfer entropy every ordered pair states, via binary occupancy decomposition. Note: states non-target source channels become redundant; prefer bivariate mode larger alphabet matters. Bivariate (y supplied): full-alphabet transfer entropy two aligned categorical series, directions.

title: Directed transfer entropy for categorical sequences (experimental) — transfer_entropy
code: transfer_entropy(   x,   y = NULL,   lag = 1L,   history = 1L,   test = c("surrogate", "none"),   R = 199L,   normalize = TRUE,   seed = NULL )

title: Directed transfer entropy for categorical sequences (experimental) — transfer_entropy
text: x Categorical sequence data: vector (one sequence), matrix / data.frame one sequence per row one time-step per column (NA-padded), exactly shape lsa() consumes. y Optional second series, shape x, bivariate transfer entropy. NULL, directed state-flow network x returned. lag Integer >= 1. Prediction horizon: target's future taken lag steps ahead. Default 1. history Integer >= 1. Order target's history conditioned (combined composite symbol). Default 1. test "surrogate" (default) runs source-permutation null give p-value bias-corrected effective transfer entropy; "none" skips . R Integer. Number surrogate permutations. Default 199. normalize Logical. Add te_normalised, transfer entropy share target's leftover uncertainty H(future | history), [0, 1]. Default TRUE. seed Optional integer seed surrogate test.

title: Directed transfer entropy for categorical sequences (experimental) — transfer_entropy
text: tidy data.frame, one row per ordered pair, columns , , te (bits), te_effective (surrogate-debiased), te_normalised (0-1, normalize), p (surrogate p-value), n (pooled transitions used). Rows ordered descending te.

title: Directed transfer entropy for categorical sequences (experimental) — transfer_entropy

title: Transitions of an LSA Fit (Tidy) — transitions
text: fit lsa fit lsa(), grouped lsa_group. significant Logical. Keep transitions whose adjusted- residual p-value alpha. Default FALSE (keep ). direction One "" (default), "" (-represented: significant positive residual), "" (-represented: significant negative residual). Selecting direction implies significant = TRUE. min_count Optional integer. Keep transitions observed least many times. Default NULL (count filter). alpha Significance threshold. Default NULL, uses alpha recorded fit (fit$params$alpha). sort Row ordering. "none" (default) keeps matrix (column-major) order; "strength" orders |adj_res|, "count" observed count, "prob" transition probability – descending, table reads strongest-first.

title: Transitions of an LSA Fit (Tidy) — transitions

Engines compute extra per-cell statistics append columns (e.g. two-cell engine adds odds_ratio, log_or, log_or_se). grouped fit gains leading group column. Row names reset.

title: lagdynamics 0.1.0 (in development)
text: Unified lsa() constructor pluggable engine registry. Five built-engines: classical, two_cell, bidirectional, parallel_dominance, nonparallel_dominance. Convenience wrappers: lsa_classical(), lsa_two_cell(), lsa_bidirectional(), lsa_parallel_dominance(), lsa_nonparallel_dominance(). Sequence-level bootstrap (bootstrap_lsa()), permutation (permute_lsa()), case-drop stability (stability_lsa()), split-half reliability (reliability_lsa()). Multi-group fits via lsa(data, group = ...), returning lsa_group object grouped transitions(), nodes(), tests(), initial() methods. Tidy edge tables S3 objects class c("lsa", "cograph_network") seamless integration cograph plotting layer. Native transition initial probabilities: transition_probabilities() returns row-stochastic P(| ) matrix initial() initial-state probabilities. -group comparison via compare_lsa() bayes_compare_lsa(). Reproducibility hooks (indices=, shuffles=) bit-identical cross-language verification.

title: lagdynamics 0.1.0 (in development)
text: transfer_entropy(): directed Schreiber transfer entropy categorical sequences. Two modes (directed state-flow network, bivariate full-alphabet two series), effective (surrogate-debiased) 0-1 normalised variants, boundary-safe pooling never lags across sequences. Sign-blind design; pair transitions() Yule’s Q direction effect. Validated equivonly/ infotheo::condinformation, independent direct double-sum oracle, exact analytic cases.
